course_description:
This course offers students an opportunity to develop a working command of ethical management.

The purpose of this course is to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the ethical issues, dilemmas and challenges faced by managers and employees in the contemporary workplace. In the course, we will discuss multiple issues that are relevant to managers working in contemporary organisations, including the nature of ethics and different approaches to ethical decision-making, the role of ethics in corporate settings, stakeholder management, advertising ethics, discrimination, and the codes of ethics at work. We will also discuss the notion of individual morality and how we can increase the likelihood that we will act in line with our values when faced with an ethical dilemma. There will be a strong focus on current ethical issues and also ethical challenges that managers are likely to face in future.

To fully benefit from the course, students will need to complete several required readings and a short online module each week before class . Required readings will be made available at Blackboard.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

In response to student feedback, team-based case assessments will occur in several seminars throughout the semester. Cases will be posted at Blackboard each week along with other relevant articles and additional material as required. Students must come fully prepared to seminars having read the material and/or completed the relevant online module before class. As much of the seminars will involve class and group discussions, simply listening to recordings will not provide you with sufficient engagement with the course material.

Past students have also expressed a desire to work with their team members to explore ethical issues in more depth. As such, a team workshop assessment task has been introduced to encourage this and also help students develop their facilitation skills.

If you cannot attend class each week and participate in the team-based case assessments and team workshop, you should not undertake this course.

course_aims: The major aim of this course is to prepare you personally and professionally for the ethical challenges you may face as an employee and as a manager in an organisational setting. You will be encouraged to reflect on your decision-making processes and behavioural responses to ethical dilemmas and value conflicts in the workplace, and to gain a deeper understanding of your personal values and motivations. Another key aim of this course is to help you identify the links between management theory and practice through the use of critical reasoning and the analysis of case studies and practical examples. You will be encouraged to draw on evidence from the scholarly literature to support your arguments and in doing so gain a comprehensive understanding of contemporary business ethics.

learning_outcomes.description: Analyse common ethical dilemmas facing employees and managers in the contemporary business environment, both individually and as part of a team.
Demonstrate a comprehensive awareness and understanding of major business ethics theories, paradigms and approaches.
Analyse the factors that influence employee and managerial ethical decision-making.
Demonstrate knowledge of the practical challenges of managing ethical dilemmas and deviance in the workplace.
Critically reflect on your own values and behaviours and your role as an agent of positive ethical change.

assessment_summary.title: Case Study Response
assessment_summary.due_date: Case Study 1 16/03/2026; Case Study 2 30/03/2026; Case Study 3 13/04/2026; Case Study 4 20/04/2026; Case Study 5 27/04/2026; The assessments will occur in class during the following weeks: Week 4, 6, 7, 8 and 9.

assessment_summary.title: Team Workshop
assessment_summary.due_date: Week 11 - Week 13; The assessments will occur in class during the following weeks: Weeks 11, 12 and 13.

assessment_details.title: Case Study Response
assessment_details.due_date: Case Study 1 16/03/2026 Case Study 2 30/03/2026 Case Study 3 13/04/2026 Case Study 4 20/04/2026 Case Study 5 27/04/2026 The assessments will occur in class during the following weeks: Week 4, 6, 7, 8 and 9.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: Each seminar topic will have a case (and additional material, as required) assigned as a required reading. Students will need to complete all required readings prior to class and attend the seminar to learn additional content (e.g., concepts, theories, models). This task will also involve critiquing an AI-generated case study analysis, which will only be provided in class. Students are only permitted to use the approved materials (provided at Blackboard and/or in class) to answer the questions. During the allocated time in class, students will receive specific questions and will need to discuss and formulate their answers. Each team must submit their responses within the allocated time. Students will also need to provide hand-written notes taken during their discussions in class. Team Formation Preliminary teams will be formed in class in Weeks 1 and 2 and finalised in Week 4. Students will only be allowed to change teams in exceptional circumstances and with written permission of the course coordinator. Marking Each case study response will be marked out of 5. The marking rubric will be provided at Blackboard. Peer Assessment: Buddycheck will be used to evaluate team members' individual contribution to the assessment task. If team members indicate that the other members generally contributed equally, all team members will receive the same mark. If team members indicate that one or more team members contributed less than others, these students' marks will be adjusted to reflect their average peer-rated percentage contribution to the workshop. Note: Students must attend class during the first four weeks of the semester to join a team. Week 4 is the final opportunity to join a team. Students who do not attend class and do not provide an adequate reason for their absence accompanied by documentation (e.g., medical certificate) will not be allocated to a team and will receive 0 for the team component of this assessment task. AI Statement: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The answers must be submitted in paper along with handwritten notes from the discussions in class.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
If a student is unable to attend class for a valid reason, they must email the course coordinator with appropriate documentation (e.g., medical certificate) within one week of the scheduled class and they will be allowed the opportunity to complete an additional case study response in class.
Late submission
Answers must be submitted in class at the specified time. Late responses will not be marked.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessment_details.late_submission: Answers must be submitted in class at the specified time. Late responses will not be marked.

assessment_details.title: Team Workshop
assessment_details.due_date: Week 11 - Week 13 The assessments will occur in class during the following weeks: Weeks 11, 12 and 13.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: In this task, you and your team will be required to analyse one contemporary ethical issue facing organisations today. Teams will be allocated topics in Week 5. If you wish to explore a different topic, you will need to seek approval from the course coordinator. Following this, you will need to develop a workshop to teach the class about the ethical issue. The workshop must be engaging, highly interactive, and facilitators must not read from a script. Further information including the marking rubric will be provided at Blackboard. Workshops will be approximately 25 minutes in length. Peer Assessment: Buddycheck will be used to evaluate team members' individual contribution to the assessment task. If team members indicate that the other members generally contributed equally, all team members will receive the same mark. If team members indicate that one or more team members contributed less than others, these students' marks will be adjusted to reflect their average peer-rated percentage contribution to the workshop. Please Note: The assessment will be recorded for marking purposes per UQ Policy. AI Statement: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Teams will be required to submit their PowerPoint slides and any additional materials one week prior to their scheduled workshop presentation date. These must be submitted at Blackboard otherwise the workshop cannot be marked.
Deferral or extension
You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
Please note that workshops cannot be rescheduled. If a team member is unable to present on their scheduled workshop date, they must provide the course coordinator with valid documentation (e.g., a medical certificate) and they will be marked on the basis of peer review.
Late submission
A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.topic: Introduction to Business Ethics In this seminar we will discuss the structure and content of the course and the learning objectives. We will also consider the nature of ethics; approaches to ethical decision-making; and define business ethics. We will also further consider the importance of identifying one's personal values and how to create an individual ethical code. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 1 and Chapter 7. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05

Stakeholder Relationships, Social Responsibility, and Corporate Governance In this seminar we will discuss the importance of stakeholders and professional ethics and students will have the opportunity to form teams for the case-based assessments. We will also discuss the process of case analysis that will be used in the course. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapters 2 and 4. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Sustainability: Ethical and Social Responsibility Dimensions In this seminar we will discuss the importance of sustainability and managing key stakeholder relationships in this context. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 3. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Emerging Business Ethics Issues In this seminar we will discuss major business ethics issues today, with a focus on unethical business practices. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 5. This week is the final opportunity to join a team for the case study response assessment task. Assessment: Teams must submit their case study response in class. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Ethical Decision-Making at Work In this seminar we will consider the nature of ethical decision-making in contemporary organisations. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 6. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05

Organisational Factors: The Role of Ethical Culture and Relationships. In this seminar we will discuss the role of organisational factors in ethical decision-making, with a specific focus on building ethical cultures and the role of situational factors at work. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 8 and Chapter 9. Assessment: Teams must submit their case study response in class. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

Advertising Ethics In this seminar we will discuss the role of ethics and ethical decision-making in advertising, especially protection of consumer rights. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard. Assessment: Teams must submit their case study response in class. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

Equal Opportunity and Discrimination In this seminar we will consider the role of equal opportunity and preventing discrimination in the contemporary workplace. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard. Assessment: Teams must submit their case study response in class. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

International Business Ethics In this seminar we will discuss what it means to operate as an ethical professional in the global business environment. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 10. Assessment: Teams must submit their case study response in class. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

Crisis Management In this seminar we will discuss crisis management as a key ethical leadership skill needed in contemporary organisations. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard. This seminar will include a final opportunity to complete a case study response assessment task (students with extensions only). Labour Day Public Holiday is Monday May 4 2026. Please check Blackboard for announcements about the rescheduled seminar. Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04

Ethical Leadership and Developing an Ethics Program In this seminar we will discuss the importance of ethical leadership and creating an ethical organisational culture. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 9 and Chapter 11. Assessment: This seminar will include team workshop presentations. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04

Technology: Ethics and Social Responsibility Issues In this seminar we will discuss major contemporary business ethics challenges relating to technology and social responsibility. Prior to class, you will need to complete the required readings at Blackboard and Ferrell et al. Chapter 12. Assessment: This seminar will include team workshop presentations. Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
Course Review The final seminar will include a discussion of major learnings from the course and examination preparation advice and tips. Learning outcomes: L02
